title: alpinia suriana
intro: Alpinia suriana, also known as the lesser Galangal or Thai ginger, is a species of plant in the family Zingiberaceae, native to Southeast Asia and Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to aid digestion and relieve nausea. The health benefits of Alpinia suriana include reducing inflammation and pain, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system. Alpinia suriana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies, such as soups and infusions, to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
